description: Round 5 hardening: calendar date bug, password reset token exposure, SSE memory leak, contact sort null handling, unused imports cleanup, cookie parsing safety, and extension listener cleanup.

userStories.title: Fix calendar date range off-by-one at month boundaries
userStories.description: backend/cal/service.py line 95: the default date range calculation uses `today.month % 12 + 1` to compute the first day of next month, then subtracts 1 day to get the last day of the current month. This is fragile at year boundaries and doesn't use the standard library. Replace with calendar.monthrange(today.year, today.month)[1] to get the last day of the current month directly. This avoids the modular arithmetic entirely.
acceptanceCriteria: Default date range uses calendar.monthrange() instead of manual month arithmetic
Events in December are correctly displayed (no year boundary bug)
Events on the last day of any month are included in the range
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: Bug — HIGH severity. Calendar shows wrong events at month/year boundaries.

userStories.title: Move password reset token from URL query param to POST body flow
userStories.description: backend/notifications/email_service.py line 80: password reset links include the token as a URL query parameter (?token=xxx). This exposes the token in browser history, referrer headers, and server logs. Change the flow: (1) email link goes to /reset-password?email=<email> (no token in URL), (2) the frontend page prompts user to enter their new password, (3) frontend reads the token from a separate short-lived cookie set during the forgot-password response, or (4) use a POST-based token submission where the token is in the request body, not the URL.
acceptanceCriteria: Password reset token is NOT visible in the URL bar or browser history
Reset flow still works end-to-end (forgot → email → reset → login)
Token is transmitted via POST body or httpOnly cookie, not URL param
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: Security — MEDIUM severity. Token in URL leaks via referrer headers and browser history.

userStories.title: Fix SSE connection memory leak on client disconnect
userStories.description: backend/notifications/notification_service.py lines 25-39: _broadcast_to_sse_connections adds queues to _sse_connections[user_id] but only removes them when QueueFull is raised. If a client disconnects without triggering QueueFull, the queue reference leaks indefinitely. Add cleanup: (1) catch asyncio.QueueFull AND check for closed/invalid queues, (2) delete the user_id key from _sse_connections when the queue list is empty, (3) add a periodic cleanup or use weak references for queue tracking.
acceptanceCriteria: Disconnected client queues are cleaned up from _sse_connections
Empty user_id entries are removed from _sse_connections dict
No memory growth from repeated SSE connect/disconnect cycles
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: Bug — MEDIUM severity. Memory leak under sustained SSE usage.

userStories.title: Handle null last_contacted_at in contact list sorting
userStories.description: backend/contacts/service.py: list_contacts sorts by last_contacted_at descending, but contacts created without a last_contacted_at value (null) have unpredictable sort position in MongoDB. Add a secondary sort key of created_at descending so null-last_contacted_at contacts appear in creation order. This ensures stable, predictable ordering for all contacts.
acceptanceCriteria: list_contacts sorts by (last_contacted_at desc, created_at desc)
Contacts with null last_contacted_at appear after contacted ones
Sort order is stable and predictable across queries
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: Bug — MEDIUM severity. Users see inconsistent contact ordering.

userStories.title: Remove unused imports in backend modules
userStories.description: Three backend files have unused imports: (1) backend/documents/router.py line 3: 'import base64' is never used, (2) backend/jobs/schemas.py line 6: 'AnyHttpUrl' is imported but never referenced, (3) backend/notifications/notification_service.py line 5: 'import json' is never used. Remove all three unused imports.
acceptanceCriteria: base64 import removed from documents/router.py
AnyHttpUrl import removed from jobs/schemas.py
json import removed from notification_service.py
All backend tests pass: pytest tests/ -x
userStories.notes: Code quality — LOW severity. Dead imports add cognitive noise.

userStories.title: Add try-catch around decodeURIComponent in getCookie
userStories.description: frontend/src/api/client.js lines 9-12: getCookie calls decodeURIComponent() on the cookie value without error handling. Malformed percent-encoded values (e.g., '%ZZ') throw URIError, crashing all API requests that depend on CSRF token retrieval. Wrap in try-catch, return null on decode failure, and log a warning.
acceptanceCriteria: decodeURIComponent wrapped in try-catch
Malformed cookie values return null instead of throwing
Warning logged to console on decode failure
npm test passes all tests
userStories.notes: Bug — LOW severity but HIGH blast radius. One bad cookie breaks all API calls.

userStories.title: Deduplicate Escape key listeners in extension content script
userStories.description: extension/content/content.js lines 86-95: injectBanner adds a new 'keydown' event listener each time it's called. On pages with multiple job listings or SPA navigation, listeners accumulate. Refactor to use a single module-level Escape handler that tracks the current active banner, or use AbortController to clean up listeners when the banner is dismissed.
acceptanceCriteria: Only one Escape key listener active at any time regardless of banner count
Listener is properly removed when banner is dismissed
Escape still dismisses the most recently shown banner
All extension tests pass: npm test
userStories.notes: Code quality — LOW severity. Listener accumulation is wasteful on SPA pages.
